The '''meditation gardens''' of the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] were lush [[Meditation/Legends|meditative]] retreats in which members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] could visit at any time of the day or night while at the Temple.

==History==
Seeing completion in the Temple no later then [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], the several meditation gardens that filled the Temple were beautifully maintained throughout the Order's history as places of relaxation and reflection.<ref name="HOF" />

Around [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]], [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Xiaan Amersu]], a young [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] girl whose [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] had been slain by [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]], found amnesiac Jedi [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] meditating on a bench beneath a great tree. Giving the [[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] Jedi her [[Heart of Fire]] gemstone, Amersu told the Jedi that his Padawan, [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]], had given her the stone, which in turn had been a gift from Vos. Amersu though it right to give the stone back to Vos in hopes that he could use his [[Psychometry/Legends|psychometric]] abilities to read the stone and regain his memories of Secura. In doing so, Vos felt that he had regained some of his past and thanked the girl before swearing that he would indeed find Secura.<ref name="HOF" />

Several years later, during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Master [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]] brought the comatose former-High Council member [[Depa Billaba/Legends|Depa Billaba]] to a garden for [[Force healing/Legends|healing]]. Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], Billaba's former master, visited the pair by the fish-filled pond and inquired as to why Saa even tried, for Windu was certain that Billaba would never wake. Saa stated that she would not give up, and her attempts at healing would heal her own wounds.<ref name="SWR">''[[Star Wars: Republic: Show of Force]]''</ref> Additionally, Jedi [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] would wait for Master [[Nejaa Halcyon]] in a grotto in one of the gardens as the latter had news to report on after a meeting with the High Council. Halcyon quickly arrived at the grotto to tell Skywalker they were being sent to [[Praesitlyn/Legends|Praesitlyn]] to oversee the defense of the [[Intergalactic Communications Center]] and capture [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zozridor Slayke]].<ref name="JT">''[[Jedi Trial]]''</ref>

At the end of the War, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], secretly the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] Darth Sidious, ordered the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] to lay [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|siege]] to the Temple and slaughter its occupants. The [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], led by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] raided the building and lay waste to much of its ancient architecture. The meditations were burned or merely left to crumble as the ancient structure was ruined and its occupants hunted down and murdered.<ref name="ROTS" />

When Sidious and Vader were slain, the Empire lost balance and was eventually [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|driven]] from Coruscant and taken by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], Coruscant was devastated by [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]], causing the Temple to crumble completed, seeing the final destruction of the meditation gardens.<ref name="ELII">''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]''</ref> Following the reclamation of the world, the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]], [[Cal Omas]], ordered the [[Reconstruction Authority]] to rebuild the ancient edifice as a gift for the great sacrifices made by the Order against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].<ref name="DNI" />

Reconstructed in the [[New Jedi Temple|New Temple]], the meditation gardens served the same purpose as they had several decades before. In [[43 ABY]] Interim [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Kenth Hamner]] summoned Jedi [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], and non-Jedi [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] to the garden in the [[Tower of Reconciliation/Legends|Tower of Reconciliation]] to meet with Chief of State [[Natasi Daala]] and her personal assistant [[Wynn Dorvan]]. During the meeting, Daala argued for the procurement of the [[Force psychosis|maddened]] Jedi [[Seff Hellin]] and [[Natua Wan]] while Hamner argued that the [[New Jedi Order|Order]] was better equipped to treat the insane Jedi. Coming to a compromise, the Jedi agreed to make public apologies on the matter concerning the clandestine group [[Darkmeld]], while Daala reluctantly agreed that the Jedi could house the two infected Jedi.<ref name="FOTJO" />

==Layout==
Situated on various levels of the Temple Ziggurat and within the four Council Spires, the meditation gardens were generally cool, sun-drenched chambers in which Jedi could meditate amongst the foliage and trickling streams. Filled with dense foliage and moist grottoes, benches lined dirt and stone paths that cut through the jungle-like chambers.<ref name="JT" /> In one of the gardens, a fish-filled pond connected to a sandy beach, while [[Butterfly/Legends|butterflies]] fluttered over the gently lapping waters.<ref name="SWR" />

On the rooftop of the Ziggurat were at least eight large gardens located in the architecture that jutted out from the pyramid of the Temple. Clerestories formed the ceilings of these gardens and access to the Ziggurat's broad rooftop plaza was gained through three broad doorways.<ref name="SWCL" /> In one of the gardens, a sandpit near a towering tree was filled with pink stones which were gently balanced upon each other, inducing a very calming atmosphere.<ref name="HOF" />
